Name: pac_bayes_latency
Title: PAC-Bayes Frameworks for Latency-Aware Machine Learning Inference
Short Hypothesis: By integrating PAC-Bayes frameworks with spectral stability bounds, it is possible to derive novel, strictly bounded-overhead protocols for latency-critical machine learning inference, thereby improving the efficiency and reliability of ML models in real-time applications.
Related Work: While existing research has explored the use of PAC-Bayes frameworks for theoretical guarantees in machine learning, and spectral stability bounds for analyzing model robustness, there is a lack of work combining these approaches specifically for latency-aware inference. This proposal distinguishes itself by focusing on the intersection of these areas to address the pressing need for reliable, low-latency ML inference.
Abstract: This proposal outlines a novel approach to latency-aware machine learning inference, leveraging PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability bounds. By developing strictly bounded-overhead protocols, this research aims to enhance the efficiency and reliability of ML models in real-time applications. Key contributions include the formulation of a Jacobian-based layer-skipping framework for minimizing hidden-state perturbation and enforcing strict wall-clock budgets, and the implementation of sequential adaptive thresholds to eliminate memory pressure. Experimental validation will focus on first-class latency benchmarks, targeting throughput-accuracy Pareto frontiers and calibrating stability metrics using uncertainty-quantified activation proxies.
Experiments: Experiments will involve: (1) developing and testing the PAC-Bayes framework integrated with spectral stability bounds on benchmark datasets, (2) evaluating the performance of the proposed layer-skipping framework under various latency constraints, and (3) comparing the efficiency and reliability of the proposed approach against state-of-the-art latency-aware inference methods. Evaluation metrics will include latency, throughput, accuracy, and stability, with a focus on demonstrating the feasibility and superiority of the proposed method.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the complexity of integrating PAC-Bayes frameworks with spectral stability bounds, the challenge of ensuring strict latency guarantees in practice, and the possibility that the proposed methods may not generalize well across different datasets and application domains. Limitations of this study may include the reliance on specific architectural assumptions and the need for further research to fully explore the potential of this approach.
